This species is classified within the genus Carex, which comprises over 2,000 species of sedges found worldwide. Carex pseudosabynensis is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the genus. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf and stem structure, and its growth habits.

intro: Carex pseudosabynensis, also known as Korean sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to Korea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in the treatment of conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
